Official GCompris website screenshots page (https://www.gcompris.net/screenshots-en.html) lists 'Draw numbers' as the Mathematics category activity with description 'Connect the dots to draw numbers from 0 to 9'. The activity name is 'Draw numbers', not 'Connect the dots'.
Verified in verifier.py lines 46-62: 15 points for screenshot (lines 47, 50) + 15 points for text file (lines 56, 59) = 30 total
Verified in verifier.py lines 94-104: VLM Workflow & Navigation scoring gives 30 points if navigation is successful (line 101), not 20

Examined verifier.py lines 1-143 thoroughly. No separate 'interaction' scoring category exists. Navigation (30 pts) and completion (30 pts) are the VLM-based scoring categories, plus file_evidence (30 pts) and content_check (10 pts).
Verified in verifier.py lines 106-131: VLM Completion Verification gives 30 points if completion is confirmed (line 128), not 20

privileged_info_summary: The task uses verified file paths /home/ga/Documents/solved_dots.png and /home/ga/Documents/object_id.txt. The GCompris activity being targeted is called 'Draw numbers' (not 'Connect the dots') and is located in the Mathematics > Numeracy category. It requires students to click numbered dots in sequence to draw numbers 0-9. The verifier implements a 100-point scoring system: file_evidence (30 pts), content_check (10 pts), VLM workflow & navigation (30 pts), and VLM completion verification (30 pts), with a 70-point pass threshold. Metadata scoring claims of navigation (20 pts) and completion (20 pts) contradict the actual verifier implementation (30 pts each).
